title: Coelorachis corrugata
uses: Coelorachis corrugata is reported to have medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. In some African cultures, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in remedies, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses. Traditional healers continue to use Coelorachis corrugata in their practices, but its use should be approached with caution until further scientific evidence is available.
taxonomy: Coelorachis corrugata is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Coelorachis. The classification of Coelorachis corrugata is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Magnoliophyta. This species is also classified within the order Poales. Furthermore, Coelorachis corrugata can be identified under the class Liliopsida and the family Poaceae.

constituents: Coelorachis corrugata contains sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids as its active constituents, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The sesquiterpene lactones in Coelorachis corrugata have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, while the flavonoids have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active constituents in Coelorachis corrugata may contribute to its traditional uses in folk medicine, such as treating fever and other health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms and effects of these active constituents in Coelorachis corrugata.

intro: Coelorachis corrugata, also known as the corrugate panicgrass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelorachis corrugata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelorachis corrugata is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to treat various health conditions, and its leaves are also used in traditional remedies for skin and wound care.
